title: The global economy keeps growing despite shocks
subtitle: Global average per capita GDP growth rates, 1960-2027
textParagraph: Over the past 65 years, the global economy has grown at remarkably steady rates, despite sustaining several shocks. On average, the world recovered swiftly after the substantial shock from COVID-19 in 2020. By 2021, average global GDP per capita surpassed that of 2019, and by the end of 2025, global GDP per capita had increased by 10 percent compared to 2019 [reference: WB26]. Looking ahead, solid growth is projected to continue, despite continued uncertainties from e.g. global trade.
